mechanism_narrative: Accommodative monetary policy proxied by a federal funds rate held near zero compresses the equity discount rate and lowers the opportunity cost of holding stocks relative to cash, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 twenty-day forward returns following months with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above nine percent signals a deep recessionary labor market that depresses consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 twenty-day forward returns are positive in more than fifty-five percent of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds nine percent over any two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in retail sales and housing starts signals a coordinated rebound in consumer and residential investment demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next sixty trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and HOUST changes in a regression fails to significantly predict sixty-day forward returns (p > 0.05) or carries a negative sign over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a month-over-month increase in industrial production from a depressed base signals normalization of manufacturing output and operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive twenty-day forward returns occur in fewer than fifty-five percent of months during post-shock recovery periods when INDPRO is rising over any three-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below one-half percent combined with a rising consumer price index generates deeply negative real interest rates that repress risk-free yields and drive capital reallocation into equities, producing positive five-day forward returns.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with inflation pressures
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and rising CPIAUCSL fails to produce positive five-day returns in more than fifty-five percent of occurrences over any two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP signals a contraction in aggregate demand that compresses corporate revenue expectations and profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Negative sixty-day forward returns follow in fewer than fifty-five percent of quarters when GDPC1 declines over any five-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and consumption capacity that reduces recession risk and supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive twenty-day forward returns follow in less than fifty-five percent of months with rising PAYEMS over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below twenty-five basis points signals an extremely accommodative liquidity environment that reduces short-term funding costs for leveraged market participants, generating positive five-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over any two-year rolling window.
